skill.name: trade-show-finder
skill.description: Find, compare, and research trade shows, exhibitions, expos, and industry events by vertical, region, date, or audience. Use this skill whenever the user wants to discover which trade shows exist for their industry, compare multiple events side-by-side, decide which shows are worth attending or exhibiting at, look up event dates and venues, research exhibitor counts or visitor profiles, or plan an annual trade show calendar. Also triggers on questions like 'what are the best shows for [industry]', 'when is [show name]', 'should we go to [event] or [event]', 'find me exhibitions in Germany for packaging', 'trade show calendar 2026', 'exhibition calendar Europe', 'B2B trade shows', 'what industry events should I attend', 'upcoming trade fairs', or even vague requests like 'we need to get in front of more buyers — what events should we be at'. If the user mentions any specific trade show by name (CES, MEDICA, Hannover Messe, Interpack, SXSW, Bauma, etc.) and wants information about it, use this skill.
skill.summary: AI-powered trade show discovery and comparison tool for industry events worldwide
skill.author: LensmorOfficial

security_audit.summary: All 40 static analysis findings are false positives from pattern-matching on markdown documentation. The skill contains only text files (SKILL.md, README files, examples) with no executable code. Detected patterns include: hardcoded URLs (legitimate trade show websites and author links), shell backticks (markdown code formatting), path traversal sequences (relative markdown links to related skills), and UTM parameters (marketing tracking). This is a safe, legitimate documentation skill for trade show research.

content.user_title: Find and Compare Trade Shows Worldwide
content.value_statement: Stop wasting hours searching scattered event directories. This skill uses web search to find relevant trade shows, presents them in structured comparison tables, and provides targeted recommendations based on your industry, region, and goals.
actual_capabilities: Search and find trade shows by industry, region, and date using web search
Compare multiple events side-by-side with dates, locations, exhibitor counts, and visitor statistics
Provide tailored recommendations based on user goals (attending, exhibiting, or competitor scouting)
Research specific trade shows by name to get dates, venues, and event details
Generate decision matrices to help choose between multiple events
Include practical context like registration deadlines, co-located events, and travel tips
limitations: Relies on publicly available information from trade show websites and directories
Event dates and statistics may change; data accuracy depends on official sources
Cannot access private or paywalled exhibitor databases
Large trade show databases may require manual verification for niche industries

use_cases.title: Find Industry Events
use_cases.description: Discover relevant trade shows for your industry in specific regions. Get comprehensive lists with dates, locations, and attendee profiles to plan your event calendar.

use_cases.title: Compare Event Options
use_cases.description: Evaluate multiple trade shows to decide which events offer the best ROI for exhibiting. Get side-by-side comparisons of audience size, cost, and relevance.

use_cases.title: Research Specific Shows
use_cases.description: Look up details about specific trade shows by name. Verify dates, venues, visitor demographics, and exhibition focus before committing to attend.

prompt_templates.title: Basic Industry Search
prompt_templates.prompt: Find packaging trade shows in Europe for 2026
prompt_templates.scenario: Quick search for events in your industry and region

prompt_templates.title: Comparison Request
prompt_templates.prompt: Compare CES, MWC, and IFA for a consumer electronics startup. Which one should we exhibit at?
prompt_templates.scenario: Evaluate multiple events to make a strategic decision

prompt_templates.title: Goal-Oriented Search
prompt_templates.prompt: We sell industrial automation equipment. What are the best trade shows in Asia to find distributors?
prompt_templates.scenario: Find events aligned with specific business objectives

prompt_templates.title: Specific Event Lookup
prompt_templates.prompt: When is MEDICA 2026 and how many attendees does it typically have?
prompt_templates.scenario: Get details about a particular trade show

output_examples.input: Find medical device trade shows in Europe for 2026
output_examples.output: Structured comparison table with MEDICA, Compamed, Arab Health, MedtecLIVE, DMEA, and Medical Japan showing dates, locations, exhibitor counts, visitor counts, and key focus areas. Includes top picks analysis with specific recommendations for surgical instrument manufacturers.

output_examples.input: Compare CES and MWC for a startup
output_examples.output: Side-by-side comparison highlighting CES strengths in consumer electronics and general tech exposure versus MWC's focus on mobile and telecommunications. Includes decision matrix: 'Biggest audience' goes to CES, 'Best for telecom partnerships' goes to MWC.

best_practices: Be specific about your industry and target region for the most relevant results
Mention your goal (attending vs. exhibiting) to get tailored recommendations
Include your timeframe or budget constraints when asking for comparisons
Use the skill early in your planning process to identify registration deadlines
anti_patterns: Do not ask for real-time ticket booking or registration—this skill only provides information
Avoid asking for historical data beyond what is publicly archived on trade show websites
Do not expect private exhibitor lists or attendee contact information
Avoid very broad searches like 'find all trade shows' without industry or region context

faq.question: How current is the trade show information?
faq.answer: The skill uses web search to find the latest available information from official show websites and industry directories. Dates and statistics are verified against multiple sources when possible.

faq.question: Can this skill book exhibition space or register me for events?
faq.answer: No. This skill only provides research and comparison information to help you decide which events to attend. You must register directly through the official trade show websites.

faq.question: Does this work for any industry?
faq.answer: Yes. The skill can research trade shows for any industry that has public events and exhibitions, from medical devices and manufacturing to technology, food & beverage, and more.

faq.question: What information does each trade show entry include?
faq.answer: Each entry includes the official name, dates, city and venue, official website, exhibitor count, visitor count, key industries covered, show frequency, and notable facts about the event.

faq.question: Can I compare events in different regions?
faq.answer: Yes. You can ask to compare events across different continents or countries to decide which regions offer the best opportunities for your business.

faq.question: How does this skill choose which trade shows to recommend?
faq.answer: The skill analyzes your specific criteria (industry, region, goals, budget, timeframe) and ranks events by relevance. Top picks include detailed reasoning about why each show matches your needs.
